mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE drop exceeding 0.5 percentage points) signals rapid labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in stronger economic momentum.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If following such UNRATE drops the SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of cases (sample of at least 10 events), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and housing starts exceed 500K units, it indicates that accommodative monetary policy is successfully stimulating the housing sector, a key driver of economic activity. This joint condition generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the transmission mechanism unfolds.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with housing recovery
failure_condition: If when both conditions hold, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances (minimum 10 occurrences), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of a manufacturing downturn, a sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) of at least 1% month-over-month signals the onset of a supply-side recovery. This improvement in production capacity and utilization drives corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when INDPRO rises >1% m/m, SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than half of such episodes, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) erode real purchasing power and signal mounting inflationary pressures, which historically lead to tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. This relationship holds continuously without threshold.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year rolling period, positive CPIAUCSL changes are associated with positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST exceeding 10% growth) signals a sudden improvement in the housing market and broader construction sector. This positive demand shock rapidly boosts related industries and investor sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If following such HOUST spikes, SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of events (minimum 15 occurrences), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent robust growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS increase > 200K) and strong industrial production growth (INDPRO > 0.5% m/m) provides a comprehensive signal of broad-based economic strength, encompassing both labor market and manufacturing sectors. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market prices in synchronized expansion.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200K and INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances (minimum 8 occurrences), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of extremely high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%), a declining unemployment trend signals that the labor market is healing from a severe downturn. This improvement triggers strong positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as markets anticipate a sustainable recovery and eventual normalization.
failure_condition: If when UNRATE > 8% and subsequently declining, SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods (minimum 5 episodes), the hypothesis is rejected.
